titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lesley Blanch Papers
titlestmt.author: by Kristen E. Muenz

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

languagedeclaration.language: The finding aid is written in English.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday July 11, 2018 at 19:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Lesley Blanch Papers contain correspondence, photographs, artwork, and other printed material by Lesley Blanch or pertaining her life and work. Also included are personal and business papers related to her writings and estate.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 20, 21 (audiovisual and electronic media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

bioghist.head: Lesley Blanch
bioghist.p: Lesley Blanch (1904-2007) was a writer, historian, artist, and traveller. Her works and journeys focused on Russia and the Middle East. She began her career as an artist, studying painting at the Slade School of Fine Art at University College London. She later became a journalist, serving as the features editor of British Vogue from 1937 to 1944. Her first book, The Wilder Shores of Love , was published in 1954. Over the course of her life, she wrote 12 books, and a posthumous collection of her memoirs was released in 2015. She was married to Robert Alan Wimberley Bicknell from 1930 to 1941, and to French novelist Romain Gary from 1945 to 1962.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lesley Blanch Papers. James Marshal and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Lesley Blanch Papers contain correspondence, photographs, artwork, business papers, and other printed material by or relating to the life and work of Lesley Blanch, as well as a small amount of material from her estate.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Lesley Blanch Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series are organized alphabetically by folder title. General correspondence is placed at the end of the series.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: For additional correspondence, see photographs, personal papers, and business papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence and related materials such as photographs and drawings.

did.unittitle: Autographed card from Lesley Blanch to George Cukor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contain letters from Edna Fleming, Gerald de Gaury, Nancy Mitford, Julian Barnes, Pat Kavanagh, and Gore Vidal.

did.materialspec: Select pieces in this series have fire damage. This is due to a fire that destroyed Lesley Blanch's house near Milton, England in 1994.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged alphabetically by folder title. Undated and unidentified photographs are placed at the end of the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains primarily photographs with some correspondence and other related papers laid in throughout.

did.unittitle: Photograph and invitation, Duke and Duchess of Windsor
Photographs of Lesley Blanch and Romain Gary

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains objects and papers related to Lesley Blanch's personal affairs, including those after her death.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material related to Lesley Blanch's career as a writer, including clippings of articles she wrote, reviews of her books, and papers related to The Sabres of Paradise (1960). Also included is legal documentation and papers related to an unauthorized biography of Lesley Blanch (authored by Anne Boston, 2010).

did.unittitle: Copy of The Seven Veils of Islam
Eastern Eye, the Selected Writings of Lesley Blanch
Materials related to New College Library donation
Research materials related to Sabres of Paradise

did.materialspec: Select pieces in this series have fire damage. This is due to a fire that destroyed Lesley Blanch's house near Milton, England in 1994.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains articles, notes, and publications collected by Lesley Blanch, with a small amount of material from after her death.

did.unittitle: Christie's Monaco: Collection de M. Henri Clarke

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains books owned by Lesley Blanch, some annotated. Also includes unbound books that may have been recovered from her library after the fire in 1994.

did.unittitle: Annotated copy of Romain Gary: Le Caméléon
Annotated copy of Three Sisters and Other Plays by Chekov
Notes de Route by Isabelle Eberhardt
Annotated copy of Constantinople by Pierre Loti
Bakst: The Story of Bakst's Life

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labelled "from Pictures for the Tsar" on original carrier. Possibly a partial copy of Photographs for the Tsar by Sergei Mikhailovich Prokudin-Gorskii.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains audiovisual materials with contents by or related to Lesley Blanch, including recordings of several memorial broadcasts aired after her death.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: These materials were removed from various locations in the collection for preservation.

did.unittitle: Interval reading: Journey into the Mind's Eye
Journey into the Mind's Eye: Passions - Proq 4 of 5 - Journey
Romain Gary: Une vie, une oeuvre

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note on tape label reads "Bad Quality."

did.unittitle: Writers' France: Writers on Paris 1920 on / The Riviera ; Lesley B. on Train Bleu and Maughan
CDs with material related to theater design
"Last Word: Lesley Blanch," CD recording of BBC Radio 4
"Une vie, une oeuvre: Lesley Blanch (1904-2007)," CD recording of France Culture

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings from Lesley Blanch's career at Vogue and materials related to the awards she received, including her appointment to the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire signed by Queen Elizabeth II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the documents for the medal of Officier de l'Ordre des Arts et des Lettres (awarded to Blanch in 2004) and the documents appointing Blanch an Ordinary of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire (2001), signed by Queen Elizabeth II.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.
